{{Infobox book
| name = House of Blades
| slug = tg-01-house-of-blades
| precedes = [[The Crimson Vault (novel)|The Crimson Vault]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-tg-01-house-of-blades.jpg|250px]]
| released = 2013
| isbn = 9780989671705
| publisher = Brand: Hidden Gnome Publishing
| pages = 292
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|The story begins with eight-year-old [[Simon]] witnessing a magical attack by [[Travelers]] that leaves his father, [[Kalman]], severely burned and his mother, [[Edina]], in a coma. A mysterious hooded swordsman rescues [[Simon]] and his unconscious mother, promising to teach him how to protect himself. Years later, during an attack on [[Myria village]] by [[Overlord Malachi]]'s soldiers, [[Simon]] struggles to care for his mentally ill mother while [[Leah]], a powerful [[Traveler]] with crystal abilities, uses her magic to intimidate the raiders. To investigate the village's midsummer sacrifices, [[Leah]] surrenders herself as a sacrifice to be taken to the capital.

Following the destruction of his village, [[Simon]] joins a group of survivors led by a young man named [[Alin]]. When the [[Traveler]] [[Cormac]], serving [[Malachi]], attacks the group to execute captives, [[Alin]] unexpectedly manifests light-based abilities and defeats [[Cormac]]. While [[Alin]] departs with [[Enosh Travelers]] to fulfill a prophecy, [[Simon]] stays behind to rebuild [[Myria]]. Seeking strength, [[Simon]] travels to the [[Latari Forest]] to find a legendary swordsman. After several failed attempts, he convinces the white-haired [[Traveler]] [[Kai]] to train him within his [[Territory]], [[Valinhall]]. [[Simon]] undergoes grueling training, surviving magical attacks and combat trials against Shadow{{!}}shadow-men and animated armor.

During his training, [[Simon]] encounters the [[Eldest]] of [[the Nye]], who reveals [[Simon]]'s lineage and offers a pact: in exchange for training, [[Simon]] must recover lost Dragon's Fang{{!}}Dragon's Fangs. After accepting, [[Simon]] gains supernatural speed and begins a series of trials, including a survival ritual in [[Orgrith Cave]]. In the cave, [[Simon]] uses his enhanced abilities to rescue two children, [[Andra]] and [[Lycus]], from predatory monsters. His success earns him the legendary [[sword]] Azura and a permanent place in [[Valinhall]], though [[Kai]] warns him of mysterious marks appearing on his skin.

Meanwhile, [[Alin]] reaches the golden city of [[Elysia]], where he learns that [[Travelers]] must prove their virtues through dangerous tests. As [[Alin]] navigates [[Elysia]], [[Leah]] is held by [[Overlord Malachi]], who uses a sentient 'Ragnarus tree{{!}}Ragnarus Tree' fueled by blood sacrifices to maintain a seal on the [[Incarnations]]. [[Simon]] discovers that [[Damascan soldiers]] are transporting captives from his home village; he confronts Captain [[Erastes]] and uses his powers to open a Gate, transporting the rescued villagers to safety. 

As the conflict escalates, [[Alin]] infiltrates [[Malachi]]'s stronghold to rescue [[Leah]]. [[Simon]], having negotiated with the [[Nye Eldest]], attempts to reach [[Leah]] in a tower but is captured and thrown by a giant lizard. After slaying the creature, [[Simon]] confronts [[Malachi]] in a magical duel. [[Alin]] arrives to engage [[Malachi]] in a violent battle, during which the goddess [[Rhalia]] intervenes to protect [[Alin]]. The confrontation ends with [[Alin]] killing [[Malachi]], but the chaos leaves [[Simon]] wounded. 

In the aftermath, [[Alin]] rescues [[Leah]], and the three travelers flee toward [[Bel Calem]]. To protect his companions, [[Simon]] attempts a desperate distraction against [[Malachi]]'s approaching cavalry, eventually finding himself trapped in a canyon bowl surrounded by summoned creatures. While [[Alin]] returns to [[Enosh]] heavily injured, [[Leah]] reports [[Malachi]]'s death to her father, [[King Zakareth VI]]. The story concludes with the revelation of [[Simon]]'s connection to [[Enosh Travelers]], prompting King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth to declare war on [[Enosh]] and order [[Leah]] to spy on the [[Elysian Traveler]].}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|While traveling through a rainy forest, eight-year-old [[Simon]] and his parents encounter two [[Travelers]] who use magic to attack them, leaving [[Kalman]] severely burned and [[Edina]] in a spirit-drained coma. A mysterious hooded swordsman intervenes, killing the [[Travelers]] and rescuing [[Simon]] and his unconscious mother. The stranger promises to help them reach [[Myria village]] and vows to teach [[Simon]] how to protect himself in the future.}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|As the village of [[Myria]] is attacked by [[Overlord Malachi]]'s soldiers, [[Simon]] struggles to rescue his mentally ill mother while navigating the chaos. Meanwhile, [[Leah]] reveals herself as a powerful [[Traveler]] by using her crystal abilities to intimidate the raiding soldiers and force their submission. To protect the village and investigate the true nature of the midsummer sacrifices, [[Leah]] commands the sergeant to take her to the capital as a sacrifice.}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|After fleeing his burning village, [[Simon]] meets a young man named [[Alin]] and joins a group of survivors hiding in a shallow cave. Their refuge is soon surrounded by raiders led by [[Cormac]], a [[Traveler]] of [[Endross]] serving [[Overlord Malachi]], who declares that the villagers will be taken to face judgment. As [[Cormac]] prepares to execute a captured villager with magical powers, [[Simon]] realizes that one of the captives is [[Leah]].}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|After the [[Traveler]] [[Cormac]] brutally executes a captive, [[Alin]] unexpectedly manifests powerful light-based abilities and defeats [[Cormac]]'s summoned lightning serpent. Two new [[Travelers]] from [[Enosh]] arrive to assist, ultimately driving [[Cormac]] away and inviting [[Alin]] to join them as a prophesied hero. While [[Alin]] departs with the [[Enosh]] travelers, [[Simon]] is left to bury his mother and rebuild his village, unaware that [[Leah]] has used her own hidden powers to transport the missing prisoners to a different territory.}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|After burying his mother and repairing his home, [[Simon]] leaves the grieving village of [[Myria]] to seek out a legendary swordsman in the [[Latari Forest]]. He encounters a white-haired man who uses an oversized blade and speaks to a doll, easily defeating [[Simon]] in several combat attempts. Despite [[Simon]]'s desperate pleas for help to rescue his captured villagers, the man refuses to assist him and vanishes into the woods.}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|After waiting in the forest for days, [[Simon]] convinces a white-haired [[Traveler]] named [[Kai]] to teach him how to fight [[Travelers]]. [[Kai]] brings [[Simon]] into his [[Territory]], [[Valinhall]], where the boy survives magical attacks from water-imps and Shadow{{!}}shadow-men while discovering that the environment itself is designed to test his resolve. Following a failed combat attempt against a leather-clad man named [[Chaka]], [[Simon]] is denied food and must rely on the [[Territory]]'s magical pool to heal his wounds as he begins his long training.}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] successfully opens a Gate to the golden city of [[Elysia]] under [[Grandmaster Naraka]]'s supervision, only to be left alone in the new [[Territory]] after stepping through. Meanwhile, [[Simon]] struggles to survive and train within [[Kai]]'s mysterious [[House]], navigating nightly attacks from Shadow{{!}}shadow-folk and intense combat lessons. After a trial by combat against animated suits of armor in the basement, [[Simon]] realizes he must continue his difficult training to grow stronger.}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|After a month of training in the armory, [[Simon]]'s master [[Kai]] abandons him with a challenge to find him in the [[library]] within two weeks. While pursuing a [[Nye]] through a hidden trapdoor, [[Simon]] is confronted by an ancient [[Nye]] who reveals he knows [[Simon]]'s lineage and leads him away. Meanwhile, [[Alin]] arrives at the city of [[Elysia]], where he meets a guide named [[Rhalia]] and learns that [[Travelers]] must prove their virtues through various dangerous tests.}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Eldest{{!}}The Eldest of [[the Nye]] recounts the history of [[Valinhall]], explaining how a man named [[Valin]] established [[the House]] and created the [[Dragon Army]] to protect it. To restore the declining power of his people, the [[Eldest]] offers [[Simon]] a pact: in exchange for training and potential access to a [[Dragon's Fang]], [[Simon]] must recover the lost Fangs and collect powerful objects. After accepting the deal, [[Simon]] inhales the Nye essence{{!}}Nye's essence, granting him temporary supernatural speed and agility that allows him to easily defeat two guards.}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|After defeating the skeleton [[Benson]] and receiving a vial of strength, [[Simon]] battles a fire serpent in the forge before being rescued by [[Denner Weeks]] and his talking advisor book, [[Hariman]]. [[Denner]] reveals that the [[Overlord Malachi]] is responsible for an upcoming blood sacrifice in [[Simon]]'s homeland, leaving [[Simon]] with only two weeks to prepare. To gain the power necessary to help, [[Simon]] decides to undergo a brutal seven-night survival ritual in [[Orgrith Cave]] to earn a [[Dragon's Fang]] [[sword]].}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|After being left by [[Denner]] to undergo a week-long trial in [[Orgrith Cave]], [[Simon]] enters the cavern and encounters a group of distraught parents whose children are missing. While navigating the dangerous, monster-filled environment, [[Simon]] uses his enhanced abilities to rescue two siblings, [[Andra]] and [[Lycus]], from a predatory creature. Finding the cave entrance blocked by massive monsters, [[Simon]] settles into a temporary shelter with the children, vowing to find a way to lead them to safety.}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|After fighting off carnivorous eels with enhanced strength, [[Simon]] leads the [[Agnos]] children through a shifting tunnel that leads to a massive chamber filled with sleeping rock worm{{!}}rock-worms. Using his supernatural abilities, [[Simon]] successfully carries [[Lycus]] across the cavern floor and up a steep wall to a moonlit exit, reuniting the boy with their mother. Despite his own terror and exhaustion, [[Simon]] hears [[Andra]]'s scream from below and plunges back into the cave to rescue her.}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|After rescuing [[Andra]] from a nest of rock worm{{!}}rock-worms by tapping into the deep powers of [[Valinhall]], [[Simon]] is found by [[Kai]] and given the legendary [[sword]] Azura and a permanent room in [[the House]]. [[Kai]] warns him about the growing chain-like marks on his skin and the potential danger of the [[Damascan]] family they rescued. Meanwhile, [[Leah]] navigates a tense breakfast with [[Malachi]], an Overlord who uses human sacrifices and mentions a mysterious '[[Tree]]' that piques her curiosity.}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]] discovers that the [[Damascan soldiers]] are transporting captives from his home village, [[Myria]], and reveals his identity to Captain [[Erastes]], prompting an immediate hostile response. Meanwhile, [[Leah]] witnesses the gruesome nature of [[Malachi]]'s Ragnarus tree{{!}}Ragnarus Tree, which requires blood sacrifices to maintain a seal on the [[Incarnations]]. After a guard is maimed by the sentient red vegetation, [[Leah]] begins to question the morality and safety of the power used to protect their realm.}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|During a chaotic battle, [[Simon]] uses his [[Nye essence]] and the guidance of a sentient doll named [[Caela]] to defend himself against overwhelming numbers of [[Damascan soldiers]]. After defeating the captain [[Erastes]] and freeing his fellow villagers from captivity, [[Simon]] faces a moral dilemma when he is asked to save the man who attacked them. Ultimately, [[Simon]] uses his power to open a Gate, transporting both the wounded [[Agnos family]] and the defeated [[Erastes]] into his own [[Territory]] for safety.}}
* '''Chapter 16:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|After a tense confrontation where [[Simon]] asserts his role as a [[Traveler]] and protects a [[Damascan]] family, he reluctantly agrees to lead the Myrian villagers toward [[Bel Calem]]. Meanwhile, [[Alin]] deceives his mentors to secretly travel through the [[Naraka]] [[Territory]] with [[Gilad]] to reach [[Bel Calem]]. Following a violent skirmish with [[Naraka]]'s tribesmen, [[Alin]] is forcibly sent through a Gate and arrives in [[Malachi]]'s stronghold, where he uses his powers to push past guards and begin his infiltration.}}
* '''Chapter 17:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|After arriving at the city of [[Bel Calem]], [[Alin]] uses his armored hound, [[Keanos]], to track down a hidden cellar in a local home. Inside, they find only one survivor, [[Rutha]], who reveals that the other captives were killed and that [[Leah]] was taken personally by the Overlord. [[Alin]] decides to enter the city alone to rescue [[Leah]], ordering [[Simon]] to stay behind and protect the remaining villagers during their retreat.}}
* '''Chapter 18:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|After a brief confrontation with [[Andra]], [[Simon]] negotiates with the [[Nye Eldest]] to find [[Leah]], agreeing to wear a ceremonial black cloak as part of their bargain. While [[Alin]] faces a fiery creature in the streets of [[Bel Calem]], [[Simon]] follows a [[Nye]] guide to a large building and attempts to climb a tower to reach [[Leah]]'s window. His climbing attempt is interrupted by a mechanical insect and a sudden attack through the window that drags him inside.}}
* '''Chapter 19:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|After being captured and thrown by a giant red lizard, [[Simon]] manages to slay the creature and confronts Overlord Malachi{{!}}Malachi Daiasus, an Overlord of the [[Damascan Kingdom]]. During their magical duel, [[Simon]] uses his speed and the [[sword]] Azura to bypass [[Malachi]]'s defenses by attacking a guard named [[Petrus]] instead. This maneuver allows [[Simon]] to break through a guarded door, where he unexpectedly finds himself face-to-face with a woman holding two children.}}
* '''Chapter 20:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|After a tense confrontation where [[Simon]] learns the truth about the village sacrifices, [[Alin]] arrives and engages [[Malachi]] in a violent magical battle. During the fight, a ceiling collapse traps [[Malachi]]'s family under rubble, prompting [[Simon]] to risk his life to rescue them. Meanwhile, the goddess [[Rhalia]] intervenes to save [[Alin]] from [[Malachi]]'s devastating fire attack by granting him a protective green shield, leaving him exhausted and vulnerable as the battle continues.}}
* '''Chapter 21:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|After [[Alin]] kills the [[Overlord Malachi]] in a violent confrontation, [[Simon]] is wounded by a knife and retreats through a Gate to safety. [[Alin]] rescues [[Leah]] from her tower, and the three travelers flee into the golden [[Territory]] of [[Elysia]] just as [[Naraka]] reinforcements attempt to follow them. After receiving help from an [[Elysian]] giant, the group escapes back toward [[Bel Calem]] on armored bears, where [[Simon]] prepares to intercept [[Malachi]]'s approaching cavalry.}}
* '''Chapter 22:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|To save [[Alin]] and [[Leah]], [[Simon]] decides to draw their pursuers away by riding his armored bears in the opposite direction. After a failed attempt to open a [[Gate to Valinhall]], [[Simon]] finds himself trapped in a massive canyon bowl surrounded by various summoned creatures and competing [[Travelers]]. Guided by [[Caela]], [[Simon]] prepares for a desperate final charge against the canyon walls to create enough of a distraction to escape into [[the House]].}}
* '''Chapter 23:''' {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Following the death of [[Malachi]], [[Alin]] returns to [[Enosh]] heavily injured and haunted by the possibility that [[Simon]] died sacrificing himself; however, [[Alin]] finds three golden-armored bears waiting in his room. Meanwhile, [[Leah]] reports the news of [[Malachi]]'s death to her father, [[King Zakareth VI]], and [[Indirial]], leading to the revelation of [[Simon]]'s tragic past involving [[Enosh Travelers]]. The chapter concludes with King Zakareth VI{{!}}King Zakareth declaring war on [[Enosh]] and ordering [[Leah]] to spy on the [[Elysian Traveler]].}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Simon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Leah]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Kai]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Malachi]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Cormac]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Overlord Malachi]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Caela]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Andra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Chaim]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Otoku]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Overlord (The Traveler's Gate Trilogy)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Chaka]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Benson]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|rock worm}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Gilad]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Lycus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Zakareth]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|water-imp}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Keanos]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Rhalia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Eldest]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Hariman]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Olissa]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Adrienne]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Kalman]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Boez]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Yakir]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Grandmaster Naraka]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Caius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Erastes]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Ansher]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Petrus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Edina]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Forest Demon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Nurita]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Orlina]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[The Wanderer]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Valin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Denner]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Andra Agnos]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Valinhall]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Nye]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Myria]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Elysia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Enosh]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Bel Calem]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Naraka]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[House]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Orgrith Cave]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Endross]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Latari Forest]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Damascan soldiers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Damascans]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Damasca]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Valinhall Travelers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Agnos family]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Myria village]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Travelers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Tartarus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|monster-infested pool}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Dragon Army]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Lirial]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Ragnarus the Crimson Vault]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[liquid steel]]}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Azura (The Traveler's Gate Trilogy)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Dragon's Fang]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Benson's steel]]}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Territory]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Nye essence]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Traveler]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[gate (The Traveler's Gate Trilogy)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Virtues]]}} • {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Incarnations]]}}

== Sources ==
* https://openlibrary.org/works/OL20714287W

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
